description: Hand-labelled memorial OCR ground-truth records for extraction accuracy evaluation (issue #121).
source: Manually transcribed from graveyard survey photographs — County Kerry and County Cork, Ireland.
notes: expected_needs_review indicates whether a human reviewer would flag the record for manual inspection (e.g., damaged stone, faded text, ambiguous lettering).

records.description: Clear grey granite memorial, early 20th century. Deeply incised lettering.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of John Murphy, beloved husband and father. R.I.P.

records.description: Limestone headstone, mid-20th century. Minor lichen growth on lower panel.

expected.inscription: Sacred to the memory of Mary Kelly. Gone but not forgotten.

records.description: Sandstone memorial, Edwardian era. Good legibility throughout.

expected.inscription: Erected by his wife and children in loving memory of Patrick O'Brien. R.I.P.

records.description: White marble headstone, 1930s. Some surface staining but lettering clear.

expected.inscription: In fond remembrance of Catherine Walsh. May she rest in peace.

records.description: Modern polished granite, 1960s. Machine-cut lettering, high contrast.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Michael Sullivan, devoted father and grandfather.

records.description: Grey granite headstone, 1950s. Family plot marker, clear inscription.

expected.inscription: Sacred to the memory of Margaret Byrne, devoted wife and mother.

records.description: Sandstone memorial, WWI era. Weathered surface with partial inscription loss on lower third.

expected.inscription: In memory of Thomas Ryan, who died in service of his country. Aged 24 years.

records.description: Black polished granite, 1979. Excellent condition and legibility.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Elizabeth Connolly, dearly loved mother and grandmother.

records.description: Limestone headstone, Edwardian era. Significant weathering — surface spalling affects lower text.

expected.inscription: Pray for the soul of James O'Neill, aged 67 years.

records.description: Grey granite, 1960s. Clear lettering with some moss at base.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Anne Reilly. May she rest in peace.

records.description: Limestone memorial, 1920s. Some surface weathering but inscription readable.

expected.inscription: In memory of William McCarthy, beloved husband of Catherine.

records.description: White marble, 1930s. Family plot, good legibility.

expected.inscription: Sacred to the memory of Catherine Collins. Aged 45 years. R.I.P.

records.description: Polished black granite, 1970s. Machine-engraved, high contrast.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Joseph Hayes, beloved father. Always in our hearts.

records.description: Sandstone, WWI era. Inscription mentions two interments.

expected.inscription: In memory of Bridget Doherty. Also her husband Thomas, died 1921.

records.description: Grey granite, 1950s. Clear lettering, good condition.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Daniel Kennedy, devoted father and faithful friend.

records.description: Limestone, WWII era. Some salt weathering at base.

expected.inscription: Sacred to the memory of Frances Doyle. Gone but not forgotten.

records.description: Modern polished granite, 1985. Excellent condition.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Christopher Buckley. Dearly missed.

records.description: White marble, 1930s. Minor staining but all text legible.

expected.inscription: In fond remembrance of Teresa O'Connor, beloved mother.

records.description: Late Victorian limestone, 1898. Significant surface erosion — year and lower inscription partially legible.

expected.inscription: In memory of David O'Malley. Erected by his loving family.

records.description: Grey granite, 1960s. Clear lettering, good condition.

expected.inscription: In loving memory of Joan O'Brien, cherished mother and grandmother.
